etymology.description: 手(爪, 才, 廾) (shǒu, 'hand') + 㚔 (qiǎn, 'handcuff') -> hand releasing restraint -> 目 (mù, 'eye') added for phonetic/semantic emphasis -> 睪 (shì, 'release')
etymology.interpretation: The character 睪 originally depicted hands being released from cuffs, suggesting the core meaning of 'release.' The addition of 目(mù, 'eye') reinforced or altered the character to also imply a connection with visual selection or discernment, aligning with its semantic development over time.
etymology.simplification: The character 𠬤 is a simplified form derived from removing additional components present in 睪, focusing on the basic release aspect.
